questions.explanation: This property of light is known as rectilinear propagation.

questions.text: The change in direction of light by a mirror is called:
questions.explanation: Reflection allows us to see images of objects in polished surfaces.

questions.text: An image that cannot be obtained on a screen is called a:
questions.explanation: The image formed by a plane mirror is always virtual and erect.
The nature of the image depends on the distance of the object from the concave mirror.

questions.text: Which type of mirror is used as a side-view mirror in vehicles?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors form diminished, erect images and provide a wider field of view.

questions.text: A lens which is thinner in the middle than at the edges is a:
questions.explanation: Concave lenses are also known as diverging lenses.

questions.text: White light is composed of how many colors?
questions.explanation: Sunlight (white light) consists of VIBGYOR colors, which can be seen in a rainbow.

questions.text: The phenomenon of splitting of white light into its component colors is:
questions.explanation: Dispersion can be demonstrated using a glass prism.

questions.text: Newton's disc appears ________ when rotated rapidly.
questions.explanation: The rapid mixing of the seven colors perceived by the eye makes the disc appear white.

questions.text: Dentists use which type of mirror to see an enlarged image of teeth?
questions.explanation: A concave mirror produces an enlarged virtual image when held close to the object.
